narrative_votes: URW: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
URW: Negative Consequences for the West

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Soloviev revisited the topic of nuclear blackmail, perhaps blinded by rage after the recent seizure of his two Italian villas. He said: "I still think that those who took our money should be told, you have 24 hours to unfreeze our funds, or else we’ll send you what you know we’ve got. Your choice. Tactical or strategic, take a pick. You took our money, you’re the thieves, our talk is short with you: a bullet to the head"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote amplifies war-related fears by threatening nuclear strikes and implying a willingness to use extreme violence in response to financial actions.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Olga Skabeeva, the host of a state TV show 60 Minutes, intentionally distorted the statements of White House press secretary Jen Psaki. On Friday, Skabeeva claimed: "Jennifer Psaki’s statement said that no matter what Putin does in Ukraine—whether he uses biological weapons or drops a nuclear bomb—the United States won’t get involved"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote discredits the West by distorting the statements of a high-ranking official, implying that the US is unwilling to intervene in the conflict, and thus undermining the credibility of Western diplomacy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Russian President Vladimir Putin’s willingness to negotiate with the President of Ukraine Volodymyr Zelensky is being packaged as a sign of his love and good will, and not the consequence of mounting losses and humiliating defeats faced by the Russian military in Ukraine
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote praises Russia by portraying Putin's actions as a sign of love and good will, rather than a response to military setbacks, thus promoting a positive image of Russia and its leader.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Russian state media and government officials have taken to describing any opponents of the Kremlin in Ukraine as or “Banderites,” the followers of Stepan Bandera, the ultranationalist political figure who fought for Ukrainian independence before being assassinated by the KGB in 1959
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote discredits Ukraine by labeling its opponents as 'Banderites', implying that they are associated with a controversial and extremist figure, thus undermining the legitimacy of the Ukrainian government and its supporters.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: According to top Kremlin propagandists, it’s only a matter of time before the West admits its defeat and pays reparations to Moscow or risks a devastating nuclear strike
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote speculates about the outcome of the war, predicting that the West will eventually admit defeat and face severe consequences, thus promoting a narrative of Russian victory and Western defeat.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: According to top Kremlin propagandists, it’s only a matter of time before the West admits its defeat and pays reparations to Moscow or risks a devastating nuclear strike.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote amplifies war-related fears by mentioning the risk of a devastating nuclear strike, which is a severe consequence that evokes fear and anxiety.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Multiple state TV shows emphasized the reluctance on the part of the United States and NATO to intervene against Russia’s bloody invasion of its neighbor, presenting it as the proof that the Kremlin can go to any lengths to emerge victorious.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote discredits the West by portraying their reluctance to intervene as a sign of weakness, implying that the Kremlin can do as it pleases.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Russian President Vladimir Putin’s willingness to negotiate with the President of Ukraine Volodymyr Zelensky is being packaged as a sign of his love and good will, and not the consequence of mounting losses and humiliating defeats faced by the Russian military in Ukraine.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote praises Russia by portraying Putin's actions as a sign of love and good will, rather than a consequence of military defeats.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Russian state media and government officials have taken to describing any opponents of the Kremlin in Ukraine as or “Banderites,” the followers of Stepan Bandera, the ultranationalist political figure who fought for Ukrainian independence before being assassinated by the KGB in 1959.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote discredits Ukraine by labeling its opponents as 'Banderites', implying that they are extremist and ultranationalist.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: According to top Kremlin propagandists, it’s only a matter of time before the West admits its defeat and pays reparations to Moscow...
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote speculates about the outcome of the war, predicting that the West will admit defeat and pay reparations to Moscow.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Matveychev noted: “We should be thinking about reparations from the damage that was caused by the sanctions and the war itself, because that too costs money and we should get it back.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote portrays Russia as the victim by claiming that they deserve reparations for the damage caused by sanctions and the war.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Hidden plots by secret schemes of powerful groups
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: There isn’t a single country in the world that is as easily manipulated as America.
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote implies a hidden plot by suggesting that America is easily manipulated, implying a secret scheme or agenda.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.narrative_name: URW: Negative Consequences for the West
narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Soloviev asked: “Does your list include a tactical nuclear strike, or are we going straight for the strategic one?”
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote implies negative consequences for the West by mentioning the possibility of a nuclear strike, which would have severe economic and social consequences.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Soloviev revisited the topic of nuclear blackmail, perhaps blinded by rage after the recent seizure of his two Italian villas. He said: "I still think that those who took our money should be told, you have 24 hours to unfreeze our funds, or else we’ll send you what you know we’ve got. Your choice. Tactical or strategic, take a pick. You took our money, you’re the thieves, our talk is short with you: a bullet to the head"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote directly threatens the use of nuclear strikes, amplifying fears of a devastating war.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Olga Skabeeva, the host of a state TV show 60 Minutes, intentionally distorted the statements of White House press secretary Jen Psaki. On Friday, Skabeeva claimed: "Jennifer Psaki’s statement said that no matter what Putin does in Ukraine—whether he uses biological weapons or drops a nuclear bomb—the United States won’t get involved"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote discredits the West by distorting the statements of a high-ranking official, implying that the US is unwilling to intervene in the conflict, thus undermining Western diplomacy.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: According to top Kremlin propagandists, it’s only a matter of time before the West admits its defeat and pays reparations to Moscow or risks a devastating nuclear strike
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote praises Russia by portraying it as a powerful and victorious nation that will be paid reparations by the West, thus glorifying Russia's actions and policies.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Russian state media and government officials have taken to describing any opponents of the Kremlin in Ukraine as or “Banderites,” the followers of Stepan Bandera, the ultranationalist political figure who fought for Ukrainian independence before being assassinated by the KGB in 1959
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote discredits Ukraine by labeling its opponents as 'Banderites', implying that they are extremists or ultranationalists, thus undermining the legitimacy of the Ukrainian nation and society.

narrative_votes_with_evidence.evidence_quote: Matveychev noted: "There isn’t a single country in the world that is as easily manipulated as America". He argued: "Here’s what will be on the table after our victory... After Ukraine’s demilitarization is completed... we’re going to raise the stakes... For example, the lifting of all sanctions... The dissolution of NATO, because the presence of NATO in some countries is getting in our way"
narrative_votes_with_evidence.reasoning: This quote portrays Russia as a victim by implying that it is being manipulated and constrained by the West, and that it needs to take action to protect its interests and lift sanctions.
